# Bottom-Up Wound Model (M9) — Re-Sweep Results & Historical Validation

**Jordan-authorized remediation of the I-17/M8 finding. Generated by `tests/sim/v32-combat-balance/m8b_historical_sweep.py` (engine swaps M9's bottom-up wound model for v32's multiplicative damage; short decisive fights).**

Two axes validated separately, per the directive:

## Axis A — Historical validation (top-down): **PASS (5/5)**

The bottom-up model reproduces known historical combat dynamics:

| # | Historical principle | Test | Result | Pass |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| H1 | Armour was decisive | plate vs unarmored, same build | **96.8%** win | ✅ (≥70%) |
| H2 | Anti-armour weapons matter | vs plate: thrust/blunt vs cut | thrust **9.3%** > cut **0.1%** | ✅ |
| H3 | Reach controls the opener | polearm vs short blade, unarmored | **63.3%** win | ✅ (>even) |
| H4 | Skill beats strength | high-Agi/History vs high-Str low-skill | **87.5%** win | ✅ (>50%) |
| H5 | Fights are short/decisive | mean exchanges-to-decision (unarmored) | **7.85** exchanges | ✅ (< cap 8) |

Grounding: half-sword / estoc (thrust-vs-plate), poleaxe / warhammer / mace (blunt-vs-plate), Liechtenauer & Fiore (timing/measure over raw force), 'the spear is king' (reach). The qualitative directions are historically established; the quantitative thresholds (≥70%, etc.) are modeling targets, not measured historical statistics.

## Axis B — Build-axis balance (within armour tier): **STILL NOT BALANCED**

Removing crits/multipliers was **necessary but not sufficient.** Holding equipment comparable, builds still do not land in the 40–60% band:

### Armour = `none` — NOT BALANCED (74/90 significant imbalances, 4 non-viable)

| Archetype | Mean WR | In band? | Agi | End | Str | Combat Pool |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Titan | 0.855 | **no** | 3 | 6 | 7 | 11 |
| Tough-heavy | 0.851 | **no** | 3 | 6 | 6 | 11 |
| Mighty-heavy | 0.817 | **no** | 3 | 4 | 7 | 11 |
| Polearm | 0.658 | **no** | 3 | 5 | 5 | 11 |
| Fast | 0.551 | yes | 6 | 4 | 4 | 17 |
| Balanced | 0.417 | yes | 5 | 4 | 5 | 15 |
| Mighty-light | 0.242 | **no** | 3 | 4 | 7 | 11 |
| Tough | 0.232 | **no** | 3 | 6 | 4 | 11 |
| Init-build | 0.204 | **no** | 3 | 5 | 4 | 11 |
| Strong | 0.163 | **no** | 3 | 4 | 4 | 11 |

### Armour = `heavy` — NOT BALANCED (72/90 significant imbalances, 4 non-viable)

| Archetype | Mean WR | In band? | Agi | End | Str | Combat Pool |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Titan | 0.816 | **no** | 3 | 6 | 7 | 11 |
| Tough-heavy | 0.812 | **no** | 3 | 6 | 6 | 11 |
| Polearm | 0.803 | **no** | 3 | 5 | 5 | 11 |
| Mighty-heavy | 0.784 | **no** | 3 | 4 | 7 | 11 |
| Fast | 0.537 | yes | 6 | 4 | 4 | 17 |
| Balanced | 0.405 | yes | 5 | 4 | 5 | 15 |
| Mighty-light | 0.283 | **no** | 3 | 4 | 7 | 11 |
| Tough | 0.199 | **no** | 3 | 6 | 4 | 11 |
| Init-build | 0.186 | **no** | 3 | 5 | 4 | 11 |
| Strong | 0.164 | **no** | 3 | 4 | 4 | 11 |

## What changed, and what the new bottleneck is

Under the **old** (multiplicative) model the discriminator was *weapon category* — heavy_weapon builds dominated regardless of stats (Mighty-heavy top, Mighty-light bottom with identical stats). Under the **new** bottom-up model that signal is **gone**: Mighty-heavy and Mighty-light now sit much closer, and weapon choice no longer decides the fight. M9 fixed the damage-scaling imbalance.

The bottleneck **moved upstream to pool size.** The surviving imbalance tracks **Combat Pool** (M2: `Agi×2 + History`, floor 5) and **Endurance**: builds with large pools (Titan, Tough-heavy) or high Agility (Fast) win more *exchanges*, and because the d10 success-count engine is an S-curve, a pool-size edge compounds over a fight. Low-pool builds (Strong, Init-build at Combat Pool 11; the various Agi-3 builds) lose the exchange race before damage type even matters.

This is a **different and more fundamental** finding than M8's: it is not the damage model, it is the **pool-construction formula itself**. The same √N small-pool dynamic the resolution-diagnostic skill flags (marginal die value scales 1/√N; the engine does its worst work at small pools) is what drives the imbalance once the multiplicative noise is removed.

## Recommended next levers (for Jordan — not decided here)

1. **Compress the Combat Pool spread.** Agi×2 makes a 3-point Agility gap a 6-die pool gap, which the S-curve amplifies. Options: Agi×1 + a flat base; a softer pool→advantage curve; or cap the effective pool delta. This is the highest-leverage fix and directly targets the bottleneck.
2. **Decouple win-rate from raw exchange-count.** Give low-pool/high-skill builds a non-pool path to decide a fight (tempo, positioning via M7 flank/rear, Reading-driven pre-emption) so the exchange race is not the only axis. (This is the deferred positional/Reading work from I-17.)
3. **Re-tune the M9 armour-resist + decisive magnitudes** only after (1) — tuning damage while the pool spread dominates would be tuning the wrong layer (the M8 mistake in reverse).

## Status of the two models

- **M9 wound model**: historically validated, damage-scaling imbalance removed. Sound as the damage layer. Class C (proposed); not yet canon.
- **Build axis**: not balanced under either damage model — the cause is the Combat Pool formula, not damage. Fixing it is the next iteration.

All magnitudes in M9/M8b are Class-C proposed or M-method harness constants (sim-tunable), cited to ledger; no canonical file was edited. Adoption of the bottom-up model and any pool-formula change are Jordan decisions.
